{"query":"2026 NBA Finals average margin of victory four points every game within five points final five minutes series stats","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1347,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_NBA_Finals","title":"2026 NBA Finals - Wikipedia","content":"The 2026 NBA Finals was the championship series of the National Basketball Association's (NBA) 2025–26 season and conclusion to the season's playoffs.
The average margin of victory was just four points.
The Knicks' playoff run has been labeled one of the most dominant in NBA history due to their 13-game postseason winning streak, the second longest in NBA history, and a record playoff victory margin of 19.4 points per game.
In his third season, Wembanyama averaged 25 points per game, 11.5 rebounds per game, and 3.1 blocks per game.
As the two-seed in the Western Conference playoffs, they defeated the Portland Trail Blazers in five games in the first round and the Minnesota Timberwolves in the Western Conference semifinals in six games.
The Knicks only scored two points five minutes into the third quarter, as the Spurs built a 14-point lead by the midway point.
They traded baskets on their next possessions until an Alvarado three brought the Knicks back to four points, 104–100, with 3:07 left.
The Knicks also led in the series for just 23.6 percent.
They also had the highest average margin of win at plus-14.9, surpassing the 1970-71 Milwaukee Bucks (14.5).
Jalen Brunson became the eighth player to win the NBA Finals MVP after winning an NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament and fourth of those to have also won state high school championship, joining Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, Bill Walton, and Magic Johnson.
"The 2026 NBA Finals prove historically clutch as Knicks win 3rd championship".
"The Knicks just proved more in one NBA Finals win than they did in the 11 before it"."},{"url":"https://www.nba.com/news/2026-nba-finals-prove-historically-clutch","title":"The 2026 NBA Finals prove historically clutch as Knicks win 3rd championship | NBA.com","content":"In the 30 years for which we have play-by-play data, the 2026 Finals was:
The only Finals series where every game was within five points in the last five minutes.
Five incredible games, even though the San Antonio Spurs won the five first quarters by a total of 57 points and led by as many as 29 in Game 4.
Jalen Brunson led the Finals with 22 points scored in the clutch (with the score within five in the last five minutes of the fourth quarter), the most clutch points for any player in the Finals in the last 15 years (since Dirk Nowitzki scored 26 in the 2011 Finals).
They had a tremendous run, knocking off the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder in the conference finals by winning Game 7 on the road.
They scored just 93 points on 97 clutch possessions (96 per 100) over those 11 games."},{"url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/data-graphics/nba-finals-spurs-knicks-margin-of-victory-data-rcna349638","title":"Data shows just how close the NBA Finals games have been","content":"These games have been unusually close.
Through the first 192 minutes of action, the teams are separated by only 8 points total, as New York leads in aggregate score 428-420."},{"url":"https://www.basketballpoetry.com/p/4-stats-explaining-the-nba-finals","title":"4 stats explaining the NBA Finals - by Mike Shearer","content":"4 stats explaining the NBA Finals
The Spurs have held a lead with fewer than two minutes left in all four games of this series.

Knicks ; 2. Spurs ; 3. Thunder ; 4. Celtics.","score":0.5809471,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_NBA_Finals","title":"2026 NBA Finals","content":"# 2026 NBA Finals. The **2026 NBA Finals** was the championship series of the National Basketball Association's (NBA) 2025–26 season and conclusion to the season's playoffs. It was the Knicks' third NBA championship, and their first since 1973. The series ran from June 3 to June 13, 2026. The series was a rematch of the 1999 NBA Finals, which the Spurs won in five games, and of the 2025 NBA Cup championship game, which the Knicks won 124–113. New York won the first two games, a first in the Finals for the team. In Game 4, the Knicks rallied from 29 points down in the third quarter, and OG Anunoby won the game with a tip-in \"Tip-in (basketball)\") with 1.2 seconds remaining. This was the first NBA Finals since 1995 in which a team won two straight road games to open the series; it was the first since 1993 in which the road team won the first three games.","score":0.5694942,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.5,"request_id":"f1292fbe-2df1-444e-9884-1f8cc3448216"}
